you run into any problems have any questions about these instructions, Ping Stolley Basecamp email him you're getting error messages, take screenshot write down the exact wording the error.
Available for all platforms (Mac, Windows, Linux) Download install like any other software.
Available for all platforms https://atom.io Download install like any other software.
lot developer technologies require XCode’s command-line tools, but they are useful their own right.
install them, you’ll need pull the Terminal app your Mac.
The easiest way this through Spotlight Search: click the magnifying glass the upper right-hand corner your Mac, and start typing Terminal; click the result that appears labeled Application.
You’ll have small white window that pops up, with line that most likely has the name your computer followed colon, tilde, and dollar-sign, e.g., janes-mac:~ There’ll also cursor, which may may not blink.
Type xcode-select --install (note the space before the two hyphens) and hit the Return/Enter key.
(You might prompted for your admintrator password.)
You should see some output fly by, unless you've installed XCode previously.
that case, you'll get error informing you that your command-line tools are already installed.
Once the script has finished running and you see the dollar-sign prompt again, type git version and press return.
You should see git version <hide>.<hide>.<hide> (Apple Git-<hide>), similar number, output the Terminal window.
Download the Windows installer for Git and double-click the downloaded file’s icon install it; it’ll probably your Downloads folder.
Choose "Use Git from Git Bash only*"
Choose "Checkout as-is, commit as-is"
Git Setup will then complete the installation; takes few minutes.
Click Finish when it's done.
Look for the Git git-bash icon your desktop.
Double-click it, and type git version into the terminal window that opens.
You should see output like git version <hide>.<hide>.<hide> some similar number.
you're running Linux, Git may already your system.
Run git version your terminal shell see there's any output.
you get "Command not found" error, Google around for the preferred method install compile Git your Linux distribution, preferably with its package manager you automatically receive updates along with the rest your system.
You'll need open terminal window (MacOS/Linux) Windows shell (Windows), and type the following commands.
Replace YOUR NAME and YOUR@EMAIL with the actual name and email you use.
git config --global user.email "YOUR@EMAIL"
When you've run those commands, run git config --global --list and you should see your name and email.
you not, re-run the commands above fix any mistakes you've made.
There are two options for installing Node.js MacOS.
Read about both before making decision.
The first, which not preferable, download and install the Mac Node.js installer from the Node.js download page.
Walk through the LTS installer, and accept the defaults.
Test that the install was successful pulling the Terminal app your Mac.
The easiest way this through Spotlight Search: click the magnifying glass the upper right-hand corner your Mac, and start typing Terminal; click the result that appears labeled Application.
the prompt the Terminal window (the prompt will look something like janes-mac:~ $), type node and hit the Return/Enter key.
You’ll see another line output similar v<hide>.<hide>.<hide>.
You may prompted for your administrative password, and answer few questions.
bunch output will fly by.
Once you’re returned the command-line prompt, onto the next step.
install Node.js, once Homebrew has finished installing itself, just type brew install node and press return.
bunch output will fly by.
the prompt the Terminal window (again, the prompt will look something like janes-mac:~ $), type node and hit the Return/Enter key.
You’ll see another line output similar v<hide>.<hide>.<hide>.
Download Node.js choosing the LTS Windows Installer (.msi) that page.
You’ll choose either <hide>- <hide>-bit.
you’re not sure whether your computer <hide>- <hide>-bit, you can right-click the Computer icon your Windows Desktop and look for the System type entry under System.
you’re still not sure, download the <hide>-bit version.
Once you’ve downloaded the installer file (it will called something like double-click and walk through the installer, accepting all its defaults.
After the Node.js installer has run, open the Windows start menu, and type cmd into the search box, which should return result for Command Prompt.
Open the prompt.
That will pull Windows shell.
will have prompt that looks like C:\Users\jsmith> (with your username instead jsmith).
that prompt, type node --version and press return.
should output line text that reads something like v<hide>.<hide>.<hide>.
that doesn’t work and you get something like command not found error, try restarting your computer and repeating the steps above.
Again, you're running Linux, assume you know what you're doing.
Google around for the preferred method install compile Node.js your Linux distribution choice.
You now can run the command http-server from any directory (folder) your file system, and will served locally for you access your web browser http://localhost:<hide>/.
That means you can easily web development without having post your work the web, and without the pitfalls ugly file:/// paths your browser you choose File Open your browser.
stop the server, hold down the Ctrl key and press That actually stops it; closing your terminal/shell window will leave running and cause you headaches.

original text:
Frequently, our members names will come the News for noteworthy accomplishments.
would like give you place announce your accomplishments.
Please send your accomplishments the webmasters for posting.
Photo's are welcome.
Emily Donaldson qualified three horses for the Region One Championships, and all three have qualified the Dressage Finals.
Riding Elaine DiMarco’s Campione she earned Championship Open Training Level out entries.
Riding Audi they won the Championship Fourth Level Open, and with Beaujolais <hide>, they were sixth Open Second Level.
Due selection procedures, Beaujolais has been given the chance the Dressage Finals along with his stablemates.
Leslie (Jill) Starliper and Utopian competed First Level and placed <hide>th out competitors.
Barbara Royer with Sumatra placed <hide>th Senior Rider Division
Fourth Level Ange Bean Danny’s Secret placed <hide>nd and Courtney Sarge Riverwind placed <hide>th Senior Rider Division
apologize missed your results, please send your results and will post them.
Sempre Fino "Hobbs" and Kelly Beaver successfully completed the Plantation CIC** September and Fair Hill International CCI** October, <hide>th and <hide>th place amateur respectively.
Kristen Felix topped field the Second Level Adult Amateur section the USDF National Finals KY. With score <hide>.<hide>% Second Level test they were only <hide>/<hide> percent ahead second place, and less than one percent ahead the fourth place rider.
Very tough competition Great Job!!
They were accompanied Caroline Stearns who rode Lady Shiraz first Intermediate and sixth Prix Georges warm classes, but missed qualifying for the national finals.
Elaine DiMarco has earned bragging rights for her young horse Campione.
was entered his first horse show the Virginia Horse Center Lexington.
Ridden Ange Bean, earned second and third Training Level Test after less than three months training.
Campione four year old Oldenburg.
CONGRATULATIONS all involved.
She has footage the freestyles and some the performances.
for finishing the year the Intermediate Adult Amateur Rider (#<hide> the Region) the Eventing Association's Year End Leaderboard!
Courtney and the Square Family are looking forward helping Kelly and her partner Sempre Fino accomplish their goals <hide>!
Each year, USDF hands out awards the National Convention GMO's and their hardworking members.
This year, FCEA/FCEA was selected receive two awards the Newsletter Category, and web site award.
“Inside the Starting Box: Finding Inspiration” from French Creek Equestrian Association’s newsletter, BALANCE (May/June <hide>), has won the award for Best General Interest Article for GMO with less than members.
This article was written FCEA president, Kelly Beaver.
“You’re only ONCE!"
with "Dressage Training Tips from Experience” from French Creek Equestrian Association’s newsletter, BALANCE (November/December, <hide>), has won Honorable Mention the category First Person Experience Article for GMO with less than members.
This article was written Louise Jordan-Beam.
FCEA will also received the award for Best User Experience for the web site the small GMO category.
Please note, USDF also awards amateur photographers for their newsletter submissions.
you have great photo submit, please send the newsletter editor.
You could win award, submissions must recommended August <hide>th.
would nice have some great article submissions.
Get the computer out and start writing.
Tami Dureka and The Duke Third year old Suitable become Dressage Horse, and Fifth the year old Materiale.
Kristen Ortt Felix -<hide>st place Suitable Become Dressage Horse year olds, <hide>st place and year old Stallions and Geldings under saddle.
BLM Championships: Ange Bean and Capitano were <hide>th Training Level BLM Championships, Danny's Secret was <hide>th the <hide>th level Championship class.
Labor Day Danny's Secret was <hide>th Level Champion Dressage Stone Tavern.
Rio and Susan Smith got fourth place the DVCTA horse trials August Carousel Park open training.
<hide>/<hide>/<hide> update the <hide> day Challenge: Corner House Farm was <hide>th the Small School division.
American Horse Council hosting competition called the <hide> Day Challenge, and Corner House Farm trying help the cause.
For more information about the challenge, visit www.timetoride.com.
farm, she will try introduce <hide> people horses <hide> days.
Top prize $<hide>k.
<hide>/<hide>: For the Fourth year row, the riders Corner House Farm have won the InterDressage.com Riding School the Year.
Louise and her students, competed from May <hide> thru April <hide> earn points the Riding School division.
FCEA member, Sandy Foster, CHF student team member.
Congrats all involved.
NOTE: This the same type competition that FCEA encouraging our members participate in.
More info can found the BRC page.

original text:
December <hide>, New York City, The five nominees for the Cinema Eye Honors Heterodox Award were announced today, honoring films that actively blur the line between narrative fiction and documentary.
For the first time Cinema Eye history, two films that are nominated for Cinema Eye’s nonfiction film craft honors are also nominated for the Heterodox Award.
nonfiction nominations announced last month Brooklyn, All These Sleepless Nights was nominated for Outstanding Cinematography and Outstanding Original Score and Kate Plays Christine was nominated for Outstanding Direction.
Both films debuted the Documentary Competition the <hide> Sundance Film Festival, and critics and audiences have debated the films’ place the fiction/nonfiction line ever since.
The other three nominees continue the celebration international cinema that the Heterodox award known for.
The Fits and Neon Bull debuted the <hide> Venice International Film Festival, Mountains May Depart celebrated its World Premiere Cannes that same year.
This marks the seventh year for the Heterodox Award Cinema Eye.
Previous winners the award are Matt Porterfield’s Putty Hill (<hide>), Mike Mills’ Beginners (<hide>), Jem Cohen’s Museum Hours (<hide>), Carlos Reygados’s Post Tenebras Lux (<hide>), Richard Linklater’s Boyhood (<hide>) and Jafar Panahi’s Taxi (<hide>).
With the announcement this year’s Heterodox nominees, all this year’s Cinema Eye nominated films and filmmakers have been revealed.
The recipient this year’s Legacy Award will announced the coming weeks.
Ten finalists for the Heterodox Award were selected voting the Cinema Eye Honors Nominations Committee, made more than international programmers who specialize nonfiction film.
The ten finalists were then viewed and five nominees were selected second round committee, composed nonfiction programmers and journalists.
The second round included Scott Macaulay (Editor Chief, Filmmaker Magazine), Hadrian Belove (Executive Director, Cinefamily), Eric Hynes (Associate Curator Film, Museum the Moving Image), Doug Jones (Executive Director, Images Cinema), Mads Mikkelsen (Programmer, CPH:DOX), Dan Nuxoll (Program Director, Rooftop Films), Rachael Rakes (Film Critic, Brooklyn Rails) and Alison Willmore (Film Critic, Buzzfeed).
The Honors Ceremony the centerpiece Cinema Eye Week, multi-day, multi-city celebration that acknowledges the best work nonfiction film through screenings and events.
The final four days Cinema Eye Week take place New York City, where series celebratory events brought together many the year’s most accomplished filmmakers.
This year’s dates are January <hide>-<hide>, with awards presented the Museum the Moving Image Queens January <hide>, <hide>.
Nonfiction film nominations for the <hide>th Annual Cinema Eye Honors were announced last month the Alamo Drafthouse Brooklyn.
full list nominees can found the Cinema Eye Honors website:

Did you miss BEA?
NetGalley the rescue!
Last week the publishing world convened New York for BookExpo America—the largest book trade fair the U.S. BEA annual event that gives publishers opportunity launch their biggest upcoming titles and meet with colleagues across the publishing industry.
NetGalley attends BEA connect face-to-face with our members and publisher clients share information about new features the site and reminders about how get the most out your NetGalley account.
This year our Concierge Team met with over publishers help them better connect with readers NetGalley.
From independent presses and authors the Big had fun hearing which books they’re most excited about, and gave them lots ideas for utilizing NetGalley their campaigns.
encouraged them customize their new Publisher Profiles—readers, ahead and “favorite” the ones you love!
Tarah, your Reader Concierge, also met with many NetGalley members.
During these meetings members had the opportunity ask questions about using their accounts, how they can utilize their account best for their specific member type and previewing needs, and also quick profile review make sure they have the best possible chance being approved publisher for titles.
These meetings also focused the NetGalley Wellness Challenge theme: “Do you book talk?” Tarah shared information about what publishers are looking for when you submit Feedback, they can see how NetGalley members are building buzz help launch new books.
Learn our tips participating our Wellness Challenge—if you haven’t signed the Pledge, there’s still time!
Pledge submit meaningful feedback for chance win $<hide> gift certificate the book retailer your choice.
Don’t miss next week’s Wellness Challenge Webinar (click register), where Tarah will review the basics leaving feedback, what types feedback are the most valuable, and common themes she came across during her meetings with readers.
Now, let’s get back the books!
Whether you were BEA person spirit, you still have chance snag the featured titles.
Check out our BookExpo America Wrap-Up where roundup titles hand-picked our publishers because they don’t want you miss them.
Plus, read excerpts from the biggest books the season (and click through full titles NetGalley) the BUZZ BOOKS (Adult and Young Adult).
Just click the covers download instantly!
Pledge take the NetGalley Wellness Challenge!
excited invite you join the NetGalley Wellness Challenge, which kicks off today!
The <hide>-week Wellness Challenge will help NetGalley members improve their “NetGalley health.” You can join the Challenge any time taking the pledge.
will advise you how to: improve your profile, learn why feedback important publishers, learn how good reviews build blog traffic and exposure, and more.
For more information about how participate please visit our NetGalley Wellness Challenge resource page!
The London International Book Fair one the key events the literary world; the whole world converging Earls Court the South West the capital sell rights, seal reputations and look into the future publishing and books.
Naturally, NetGalley was there meet members and clients both well get the inside track what’s new.
The NetGalley stand was right the heart the Digital Zone once niche area within the vast aircraft hangar space Earls Court, but now probably the most vibrant, diverse and popular destination the fair.
Situated next the AuthorZone lecture theatre co-sponsored NetGalley publisher Troubador were lucky hear series fascinating discussions and talks about how writers can thrive the digital age.
Many independent publishers and authors stopped off speak with their way back from the stage which was hugely interesting.
Among the personal highlights were catching with new and old publishers and members NetGalley.
especially enjoyed meeting Georgia and Samuel from www.readgig.com and you can check out their blog about the fair here.
Most excitingly all was the news that several publishers confirmed the fair that they will soon joining NetGalley.
can’t tell you who they are yet, but we’ll sure announce just soon can!
Next month it’s the turn New York play host the literary world Book Expo America.
Please stop Booth #DZ<hide> and say hello Kristina, Susan, Lindsey and Tarah!
you’re blogger attending BEA the Book Bloggers Conference, don’t miss out out the NetGalley Wellness Challenge!
you’ll NYC for BookExpo America, want meet you!
NetGalley will attending the BEA Bloggers Conference Wednesday, May <hide>th, well the rest the annual BookExpo America tradeshow through June <hide>st.
Read for full details, including how you can sign for face-to-face meeting with me, your Reader Concierge!
can’t wait see you.
Register attend BEA Bloggers Conference learn, inspired, and connect with book bloggers, authors, and publishing industry professionals.
You will benefit from jam-packed day education, extreme networking, and the passion and fun that surrounds book blogging.
Last year’s BEA Bloggers Conference was huge success with over <hide> attendees, and the BEA team eager build this success!
BEA Bloggers Conference will once again take place the day before BEA, Wednesday, May <hide>th (the BEA exhibit floor opens Thursday, May <hide>th).
Will Schwalbe will the Opening Keynote speaker <hide>:<hide>am, and Randi Zuckerberg will the Closing Keynote speaking <hide>:<hide>pm.
The conference will also feature other notable panelists over the course the day.

Cro-a-sail, Croatian racing association that aims give top Croatian sailors the opportunity compete amongst the best the world, amongst just handful organisations that have far taken delivery the <hide>.
They’ll pitting their wits against Russell’s team the Biograd match race.
For more information the Biograd race and Boat show Marina Kornati, read more about the yacht and races <hide>, and find out more about the Croatian challengers, Cro-a-sail.
Croatia Online Air Travel News The Zadar Region Opens Up?
have reasonably good authority that Ryanair will soon announcing flights Zadar.
The attention given Split Airport, British Airways and the low cost airlines, has created huge surge interest the Split Area.
Even though Zadar only two hour drive away from Split, the Zadar region has suffered result.
The availability cheap flights and the proximity airports are big factors second home purchases and also, for example, deciding where pick charter boat, this news can only good for tourism and related activities around Zadar.
Zadar itself has lot offer and the islands around Ugljan, Pašman, Dugi Otok and the Kornati islands are much more “Croatia once was” than, for example, Brać and Hvar.
Pending the announcement the schedules, you have some spare time, take the coast road from Split Zadar, via Rogoznica, Primošten, Šibenik and Biograd and find out what you’ve been missing!
Readers our last posting will aware that our blogger hosts were having some troubles with picture loading.
The problem seems have been solved here are couple pictures with the previous posting Hotel Pasike and the new language school Trogir.
See below for more information.
new language school has opened Trogir, co-owned the very dedicated and patient Ivana Rogulj, who has been assisting Croatia Online’s editor the art learning Croatian.
It’s called Katedra and located classic building the south west corner the Cathedral Square Trogir.
Apart from teaching Croatian, the school offers courses English, Italian, German, French, Russian and Spanish.
Katedra, Gradska <hide>, Trogir, well signed from the square check out you are the area and need new challenge for the autumn.
More details soon.
Just the new pedestrian bridge, the mainland sign town, Hotel Pašike and the restaurant the same name deserve full marks for trying offer something new.
The restaurant has been around for while but had facelift <hide> when the owners decided convert their old townhouse into family hotel.
The hotel bedrooms are tastefully furnished with antiques but offer all mod cons.
Prices vary from <hide> kunas for double room <hide> kunas for the apartment based people sharing the high season.
The restaurant has large and colourful terrace with tables and chairs draped gold, green, brown and yellow covers.
Waitresses are dressed traditional costume, listened great guitar and mandolin duo, and the varied menu has number traditional local dishes.
was packed when visited Saturday night and that’s probably not the best day the week try out new place the two very friendly waitresses seemed little overstretched, the food was rather than great, and it’s little pricier than many its neighbours.
However the customers seemed enjoying themselves and there was constant stream new arrivals for the hotel.
www.hotelpasike.com for more information.
seems that the Croatian Government looking regulate real estate agencies quite severely the coming months.
one month course and exams will amongst the requirements necessary gain licence.
The details are still being ironed out we’ll updating you this once have more information.
Hopefully the weather where you are better than the weather the Split area for the last few days thunder, lightening, strong south winds and heavy rain.
Looks like there more come but we're not complaining too much as, just before, had over week almost perfect summer weather and lovely warm Adriatic swim in.
The main tourist season seems have ended dramatically started.
One day the beaches are packed and the next day virtually empty.
will interesting see there are enough seasonal bars and restaurants open cater for the continuing high quantity international flights.
The stormy August weather and airport security problems can’t have helped the tourist industry statistics, and anecdotal evidence suggests that those the front line may disappointed with the success this year’s season far.
However it’s difficult judge whether that’s because expectations were much higher this year, the takings were really down, Croatians the tourist industry share similar pessimistic disposition farmers!
The statistics will provide some harder evidence due course but, with some tourism still operating under grey market conditions, statistical evidence can only partially conclusive.
We’re the process trying gather range facts and figures, well more anecdotal feedback, and will report back when can draw some meaningful conclusions.
the meantime we'd delighted hear feedback from readers.
